questions.name: Alt text
questions.question_text: An online campus pantry page shows an informative image of a shelf stocked with canned food. Which alt text is strongest?

answers.text: Shelves stocked with canned food and boxed goods in the campus pantry
answers.html: Shelves stocked with canned food and boxed goods in the campus pantry

answers.text: Leave alt empty even though the image conveys content
answers.html: Leave alt empty even though the image conveys content

questions.name: Deployment
questions.question_text: Testing a deployed version of a site is important because production behavior can differ from local behavior.

questions.name: Quality
questions.question_text: A strong final quality pass should include:

answers.text: Accessibility checks, responsive review, and link testing
answers.html: Accessibility checks, responsive review, and link testing

questions.name: lighthouse-accessibility-failure
questions.question_text: A Lighthouse audit gives a page an Accessibility score of 62. The most likely cause listed in the report is:

answers.text: Form inputs are missing associated label elements, or images are missing alt attributes — these are the most common Lighthouse accessibility failures
answers.html: Form inputs are missing associated label elements, or images are missing alt attributes — these are the most common Lighthouse accessibility failures

answers.text: The page uses too many CSS custom properties
answers.html: The page uses too many CSS custom properties

answers.text: JavaScript runs before the DOM is ready
answers.html: JavaScript runs before the DOM is ready

answers.text: The site is not deployed over HTTPS
answers.html: The site is not deployed over HTTPS

questions.name: vite-github-pages-base
questions.question_text: A developer deploys a Vite app to GitHub Pages at https://user.github.io/my-repo/. Assets load on the homepage but all images and scripts are missing on sub-pages. What is the most likely fix?

answers.text: Add base: '/my-repo/' to vite.config.js — without it, Vite generates absolute paths from / which break under a subdirectory
answers.html: Add base: '/my-repo/' to vite.config.js — without it, Vite generates absolute paths from / which break under a subdirectory

answers.text: Delete the dist/ folder and rebuild
Change all import statements to use http:// URLs
answers.html: Change all import statements to use http:// URLs

answers.text: Enable HTTPS in the GitHub repository settings
answers.html: Enable HTTPS in the GitHub repository settings
